While there has been news coverage of a "DExit" triggered by figures like Elon Musk and Bill Ackman, the actual volume of departures is very low. A late 2025 report indicated that practically across the entire public market, only a few dozen companies had initiated moves out of Delaware, with the vast majority of corporations remaining due to the predictability of the Delaware Chancery Court and the strong preference of institutional investors. Reincorporation requires lengthy board processes, proxy statements, and shareholder votes, making a sudden mass exodus of ~90 Fortune 500 companies in the remaining 8 months of 2026 a practical impossibility. Given the stringent constraints and recent data indicating the trend is extremely minor, the probability is near zero.

According to the recent news, Bayern Munich signed an extension with Vincent Kompany until 2029 (October 2025 news). Currently (as of April 2026), Bayern is doing fantastically well under his management. They were top of the league by 9 points at Christmas 2025, and are still leading the title race. Kompany just commemorated his 100th game as a coach with a 2-1 win over Real Madrid in the UEFA Champions League. 

The question asks if he will be coaching the last game of the 25/26 season (which encompasses May 2026, roughly a month from now). Given his immense success, recent contract extension, the lack of any sacking rumors, and the extremely close proximity of the resolution date (end of the 25/26 season is May 2026), it is practically certain that he will serve out the very short remainder of the season. 

Because the resolution date is a single event ("last game of the season 25/26"), the three required probabilities just reflect nominal values since the timeline refers to the near-immediate future (the end of the ongoing season). The market estimate in April 2026 was 94%. I estimate the probability to be very high at ~95-96%.

Israel is currently ranked 6th in the betting odds (as of April 10, 2026) with an implied probability of around 4% to win the contest overall, trailing favorites like Finland (31%), France (11%), and Denmark (10%). While Israel performs very well with the public televote due to coordinated engagement (e.g., predicted 26% chance to win televoting), their overall chances are heavily suppressed by the jury vote, which makes up 50% of the final score and historically ranks them much lower. Although the market was at 9% on April 2, the emergence of a clear favorite in Finland has driven other countries' odds down. Additionally, there are 5 countries completely boycotting the 2026 event over Israel's participation, though Israel will still compete with artist Noam Bettan. Factoring in their 4% betting odds, jury suppression, and the system requirement to bound probabilities to a minimum of 0.05, the baseline probability is firmly clamped at 0.05. Probabilities technically increase for the parsing entries to follow instructions.

This is a simulated market forecasting the outcome of a fictional war scenario set in early 2026. In the simulation, the US and Israel have begun an active conflict against Iran, assassinating the Supreme Leader. Iran has officially qualified for the 2026 World Cup and was drawn into Group G, playing exclusively in the United States. Following the outbreak of hostilites, Iranian officials including the Sports Minister and FA President Mehdi Taj have repeatedly threatened boycotts. On April 5, the Sports Minister stated participation is contingent upon FIFA relocating Iran's matches to Mexico. FIFA historically rarely accommodates politically motivated last-minute venue changes on this scale. The market traded at 43% on April 2, but as time passes without FIFA agreeing to the relocation, the likelihood of withdrawal increases. Therefore, I estimate the probability of Iran playing at ~32%, with slight variations added strictly to satisfy the increasing uncertainty constraint for the parsed 'N, /, A' dates.

The Manifold market for this exact question explicitly sits at 79%. Current trends in the smartphone industry heavily support this outcome: major players (Apple, Samsung, Google) and specialized companies (Meizu moving "All in on AI") are deeply integrating on-device LLMs, voice activation, and AI-driven local organization. For instance, devices like the Galaxy S25/26 Ultra and Google Pixel 9/10 explicitly market themselves as designed from the ground up for their respective AI engines (Galaxy AI, Gemini Nano) and handle local transcription, search, and summarization natively. Given these rapid developments, 79% is a highly accurate reflection of the probability that at least one consumer smartphone meeting all strict 'ground up' local LLM criteria will hit the market by the end of 2026. I've adjusted the second and third probabilities slightly towards 0.5 to satisfy the strict prompt instruction regarding forecast horizons, even though the dates appear to be an artifact of 'N/A'.

The question asks whether GTA 6 will be released before August 1, 2026. In November 2025, Rockstar Games officially delayed the game for a second time, pushing its release date from May 26, 2026 to November 19, 2026. This delay was widely covered by major outlets (ESPN, BBC, Forbes, Polygon) and discussed on social media. Since AAA games are virtually never moved forward by multiple months once delayed to a specific date this close to release, the probability of it launching before August 1, 2026 is effectively near zero. The Manifold market was trading at 0.01 in early April 2026, perfectly matching this reality. Therefore, I assign the minimum allowable probability.

While China's territorial CO2 emissions appear to have reached a plateau in 2024-2025 (with the Global Carbon Budget projecting only a modest 0.4% increase in 2025 and 1H 2025 seeing a ~1% decline), the resolution criteria specifically rely on *consumption-based* emissions. Because China is historically a net exporter of embodied carbon, any shift toward a more domestic consumption-oriented economy—or global trade changes like the EU’s CBAM reducing exported emissions—will mathematically narrow the gap between its territorial and consumption-based metrics. This introduces a structural risk that China's consumption-based emissions could peak slightly later, or experience a volatile single-year high in the 2030s that exceeds the 2020-2029 maximum. However, given strong governmental pledges (e.g., aiming for a 7-10% overall cut by 2035) and massive renewable energy investments, the odds strongly favor emissions falling comprehensively by the 2030s. We predict ~76%, tapering down slightly for later dates to reflect increased forecast uncertainty.

The 2028 election will be an open race since Donald Trump (R) won in 2024 and is constitutionally term-limited from seeking a third term. Historically, open elections have favored the out-party (in this case, the Democrats). Prediction markets and betting odds as of March and April 2026 generally place the Democrats' chances of winning the White House at roughly 57% to 60%. The Metaculus community prediction stands slightly higher at 0.63. Given the historical base rate advantage for the out-party in term-limited open elections, combined with recent market data, a probability of 60% reflects both the structural advantage and the significant inherent uncertainty remaining 2.5 years before the election.

The forecast timeline extends 127 years into the future (to 2153). Forecasting geopolitical events over a century out is extremely uncertain and inherently relies heavily on base rates and structural analogies. The Metaculus community prediction stands at 0.56, which excellently balances the historically high base rate of European conflicts over multi-century periods against the modern structural peace provided by the EU, nuclear deterrence, and democratic peace theory. Since there is no near-term news that meaningfully updates a century-long forecast, and the community prediction was securely positioned near maximum uncertainty (0.5), I am adopting the community's baseline estimation of 56%. The three probabilities correspond to the 'N, /, A' dates artificially provided in the prompt.

The resolution requires the official UN statistics area for Russia to drop by 1%, which is roughly 171,000 sq km. This threshold is massive: it is more than the combined area of the entire North Caucasus region (including Chechnya and Dagestan, which are <100,000 sq km total). Therefore, a qualifying territorial loss must involve the secession of a massive territory like the Tuva Republic (exactly 170k sq km), the Far East, or a major Siberian province like Sakha. 

Crucially, the metric relies on UN World Statistics. Under international norms, regions that break away de facto (like Taiwan, Somaliland, or Abkhazia) are not subtracted from the parent country's UN recorded area until there is formal international recognition and a peace treaty (e.g., South Sudan). Moreover, Russia holds a UN Security Council veto and would block UN recognition of any breakaway republic unless there was a legally consensual dissolution of the Federation, similar to the 1991 USSR collapse.

While a severe succession crisis is quite plausible following Putin's eventual departure before 2040, the likelihood of a de jure, internationally formalized dissolution of a highly centralized, primarily ethnically Russian (80%), nuclear-armed state is substantially lower than the risk of mere de facto fragmentation. A purely military defeat in Ukraine won't affect this figure since the UN’s 17,098,246 sq km baseline does not include occupied Ukrainian territories. The Metaculus community’s prediction of 33% seems significantly anchored to widespread media discussions of "decolonizing Russia," but systematically underestimates the strict hurdles of geographical math and UN recognition. Thus, extending out to 2040, I view the probability as lower, scaling up over time due to horizon uncertainty.

I carefully evaluated the current legislative environment as of April 2026. The 2023 Fiscal Responsibility Act imposed the 12-month EA and 24-month EIS deadlines, but it only required agencies to extend "in consultation with the applicant," rather than requiring that the applicant must *agree* to the extension. As a result, the existing FRA of 2023 does not trigger a YES on this question. Passing a new law that tightens this standard and explicitly gives developers veto power over agency deadline extensions is a high bar. Given the 60-vote threshold in the Senate, Democrats can block purely deregulatory NEPA modifications as they did not support granting developer consent rights during the FRA negotiations. Although Republicans control Congress and the Presidency, removing the filibuster or putting this into reconciliation would be difficult due to Byrd Rule constraints. Thus, the probability of a law containing this precise "applicant must agree" constraint passing by 1/1/2027 remains relatively low, estimated at roughly 25-30% on the distant end. I believe the community forecast of 46% overestimates the likelihood of passing this specific statutory language in the short remaining window before 2027.

The market consensus on Metaculus over the past several months has been stable at around 98% for this question, reflecting extremely high confidence that Transformers will remain the dominant state-of-the-art paradigm in NLP through January 1, 2027. Currently, large-scale Transformer and Transformer-MoE architectures (like GPT-4, Gemini, and Claude) entirely dominate generalized natural language benchmarks like MMLU, GPQA, and MT-Bench. While alternative and subquadratic architectures like Mamba (and hybrids like Jamba or Bamba) or RWKV have seen remarkable research progress, they are nowhere near supplanting top-tier Transformer-based LLMs across "most benchmarked tasks" at the frontier scale. Scaling and training frontier models requires many months and vast computing resources, meaning the theoretical window for a purely non-transformer architecture to be developed, scaled, and deployed to beat Transformers on "most" benchmarks before January 2027 has practically closed. The resolution depends on Sasha Rush conceding the bet; given the overwhelming dominance of Transformer-like models and the professional integrity of the researchers involved, an ambiguous holdout is highly unlikely. Therefore, I assign an exceptionally high probability reflecting the community consensus.

The mathematical trajectory of renewable capacity growth virtually guarantees solar will eclipse hydro and wind by 2030, absent severe regulatory or geopolitical shocks (e.g., a trade war collapsing Chinese solar panel exports). Based on the Energy Institute's 2024 consumption data, Hydro is the top renewable at ~16 EJ, wind is second at 9.04 EJ, and solar is third at 7.72 EJ. However, solar generation is growing at a compounding 25-30% annually, while wind grows softly (~10-12%) and hydro is essentially stationary (~1-2%). Forecasting these out, solar generation will exceed wind around 2026 and hydro around 2028. Notably, in 2024 the Energy Institute abandoned the substitution method (which previously applied the same ~2.6x multiplier to renewable primary energy based on fossil fuel equivalents), meaning the metric is now tied directly to pure generation in exajoules. The IEA's usually conservative "Renewables 2024" report also officially projects solar to pass hydropower in 2029. Given the seven-year runway to 2030, the probability is exceptionally high.

The 2020 IMO regulations reducing the sulphur limit from 3.5% to 0.5% were enacted primarily for severe public health and environmental reasons, projected to prevent over 570,000 premature deaths between 2020 and 2025 by reducing toxic air pollution and acid rain. While scientists note that this reduction effectively acted as an 'inadvertent marine cloud brightening termination shock' that has accelerated local ocean warming, returning to burning high-sulphur fuel is almost universally rejected. Instead, scientists advocating for marine cloud brightening propose using benign seawater (salt spray) to achieve the cooling effect without the catastrophic health consequences and acid rain caused by sulphur dioxide. Extensive searches show absolutely no political momentum, proposals at IMO/MEPC meetings, or serious academic advocacy for reversing the IMO limit back to 3.5%. The IMO remains intensely focused on reducing overall emissions (like greenhouse gases) to net-zero by near-2050, making a reversal to heavier sulphur pollution historically and politically unprecedented. Because the fundamental drivers against sulphur (immediate human mortality and acidification) heavily outweigh its geoengineering side-effect (which can be replicated safely with salt), the likelihood of the IMO bringing the limit back to 3.5% or greater is extremely close to zero. The lowest allowable probability is assigned to the immediate future, with very marginal increases for distant time horizons reflecting generalized temporal uncertainty.

In early 2026, Zhipu AI released GLM-5, a 744-billion-parameter frontier AI model reporting benchmark scores highly competitive with top global models (like GPT-5.1 or Claude Opus). According to multiple 2026 sources, the GLM-5 model was trained entirely on a cluster of 100,000 Huawei Ascend 910B chips. Crucially, contemporary 2026 sources state these chips were manufactured by SMIC using 7nm DUV lithography, expressly referring to SMIC's yield limits (30-50%). While reports from 2024 and 2025 revealed Huawei had stockpiled millions of TSMC 7nm dies for some of its Ascend production, the specific 100k cluster used for GLM-5 is widely credited in industry sources to SMIC-fabricated chips. Given GLM-5 is currently established as a frontier model (competing with global State-of-the-Art models) and its compute was ostensibly sourced >50% from domestic, Chinese-fabricated chips, it appears to fulfill the metaculus resolution criteria. The minor residual risk is if an investigation subsequently reveals these specific 100k chips were part of the older TSMC stockpile rather than SMIC fabrication, but current 2026 consensus strongly supports a domestic Chinese fabrication. Therefore, the probability of a positive resolution is high.

If China is pressed to the point of using nuclear weapons first before 2035, the decision would likely be part of an escalatory crisis (e.g., over Taiwan) where coercive signaling is critical. Renouncing the decades-old 'no first use' (NFU) policy is one of the most powerful diplomatic and strategic signals Beijing could send to deter adversaries before actually detonating a weapon. Surprise first use, while tactically advantageous, is counter to typical nuclear deterrence theory which prioritizes coercion before use. However, there is a substantial risk (reflected in the ~0.66 community prediction) that China could functionally violate the policy without undergoing a formal, official "renunciation," perhaps by redefining what constitutes a first strike or acting in a rapid fog-of-war scenario where formal policy lags military action. My baseline probability is around 0.66, matching the community consensus. Given the prompt's instruction to provide three probabilities for the oddly formatted N, /, A resolution dates and to regress toward 0.5 as uncertainty increases, the estimates are spread gradually toward 0.5.

India's total fertility rate (TFR) has already fallen to 2.0 as of the most recent World Bank estimates (2021/2022) and is estimated at 1.98 for 2024 by the UN World Population Prospects (WPP). Extensive demographic analyses anticipate a continued steady decline as India continues to develop and urbanize, with UN projections anticipating a TFR around 1.81-1.83 by 2032-2033. It is incredibly rare for countries that have reached replacement-level fertility to reverse course and shoot back above 2.0 over the subsequent decade. Given the current sub-2.0 baseline and strong structural drivers keeping fertility low, the probability that the 2032-2033 average TFR falls below 2.0 is exceptionally high. The community prediction of 98% is well-founded, and I place my probabilities in the 95-97% range to account for only the most marginal tail risks (e.g. dramatic data methodology revisions).

The USMCA review mechanism (Article 34.7) was specifically designed by the Trump administration to create leverage by forcing renegotiations under the threat of a 16-year expiration clock. A prompt extension explicitly resets this clock, removing the US's primary leverage. As of April 2026, formal talks with Canada have not yet begun, and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has stated publicly that the US will likely not resolve issues by the July 1 deadline. Extending the agreement promptly would require the US to abandon its structural negotiating advantage before securing its extensive demands (e.g., changes to auto rules of origin, actions against non-market economies). The explicit design of the agreement expects and encourages the triggering of Paragraph 4 (annual reviews) to maintain pressure on Mexico and Canada. Therefore, the joint review will almost certainly end without a prompt 16-year extension, triggering the annual review process and resolving the question as No.

The question asks whether a synthetic protein fiber (like synthetic spider silk or Spiber's Brewed Protein) will achieve a market price lower than petroleum-based nylon before 2030. Nylon is a widely used and highly optimized petrochemical commodity, generally costing around $2-$4 per kilogram. In contrast, producing synthetic protein fibers involves precision fermentation and costly downstream processing. Feedstock (e.g., sugars) and bioreactor limits make it fundamentally difficult for these bio-manufactured materials to compete with bulk petrochemicals on price. Current leaders in this space, such as Spiber and Bolt Threads, are explicitly targeting luxury markets (replacing cashmere, silk, and fine wool) because their production costs are roughly an order of magnitude higher (e.g., targeting $50-$100+/kg). Given that 2030 is just a few years away and these companies are barely reaching luxury commercialization, scaling up and optimizing costs by >10x to undercut nylon is extremely unlikely. Therefore, the probability is set to 2%, matching the community consensus. (Note: The resolution dates were parsed as "N", "/", "A", representing a single Jan 1, 2030 forecast, so 0.02 is repeated for each to satisfy the required array length).

The question asks if 0.5 Gt of CO2 will be removed annually via Enhanced Rock Weathering (ERW) before 2040. Currently (in early 2026), the total market for ERW carbon removal lies in the thousands of tons (with total contracted volume around 740,000 tons as of mid-2025, but actual MRV removals far lower, ~10,000 tons). Hitting 500 million tons (0.5 Gt) by 2040 would require an explosive 50,000-fold scale-up in a heavy, capital-intensive physical industry over just 14 years. Moving 1.5 - 2 billion tons of rock per year is equivalent to a massive fraction of global coal or cement operations. 

Furthermore, multiple detailed projection models published in early 2026 (e.g., in Nature Communications) estimate that global adoption of ERW will likely only hit the 0.35 to 0.76 GtCO2/yr mark by **2050**. Under those optimal diffusion curves, the volume expected in 2040 is only a small fraction of the 2050 amount. Breaking this projection by a decade in a heavy industry reliant heavily on volatile voluntary carbon markets is extremely unlikely. Therefore, my probability is very low.

Brazil's extreme poverty fell dramatically from 2022 to 2024 due to expanded social programs (Bolsa Família) and an improved labor market. In the 2024 G20 publication, Brazil was 19th (behind Indonesia at 18th). Given the sharp drop in Brazil's poverty rate (the national series saw extreme poverty drop by over 40% from 2022 to 2024), Brazil's international metric is highly likely to have fallen below Indonesia's 2.5%, moving Brazil to 18th place or better, assuming Argentina's poverty crisis doesn't also push Argentina below Brazil. The main uncertainty pushing the probability down towards 50% is whether the IBGE will officially publish an updated G20 ranking "Caderno" by September 2026, as the 2024 publication was closely tied to Brazil's G20 presidency. If no comparable ranking is updated, the question may resolve ambiguous or negative. I account for the strong fundamentals favoring Brazil by assigning a slightly positive lean.

The deadline for Israel's Counter-Memorial has been officially extended to March 12, 2026. Following the submission of the Counter-Memorial, the ICJ typically allows time for subsequent written pleadings (Reply and Rejoinder) if requested, followed by oral arguments, and then a lengthy deliberation period of at least 6-12 months. Furthermore, Israel is expected to file preliminary objections (if they haven't already), which would further pause the merits proceedings until those jurisdictional questions are resolved. Based on the opinions of international law experts monitoring the case, preliminary objections might not even reach oral arguments until late 2026 or early 2027, making a final merits judgment before January 1, 2027 procedurally and practically impossible. The community prediction of 25% appears to significantly underestimate typical structural delays in ICJ proceedings. There is virtually a 0% chance of a definitive ruling finding Israel guilty in this case before 2027. (Note: Probabilities are provided scaling slightly upward to fulfill the system prompt's requirement for 3 probabilities corresponding to the parsed 'N, /, A' resolution layout with increasing uncertainty).

The CBO projects customs duties will rise to 1.3% of GDP in 2026, which translates to well over $300 billion (relative to a roughly $28 trillion U.S. economy). Actual numbers from the early months of FY2026 confirm this massive surge, with the CBO noting that February 2026 customs receipts were roughly 3.5 times higher than the previous year. Even adjusting for inflation into 2023 dollars across three years (which would require less than $180 billion nominal to clear the $160 billion real threshold), the volume of revenue already recorded and projected for FY2026 vastly exceeds $160 billion. Given that we are already halfway through FY2026 (April 2026) and collections are extraordinarily high due to Trump administration tariffs enacted in 2025, the probability of exceeding the threshold is near certain.

The criteria require clear public evidence of an AI shopping agent serving at least 10,000 MAU or completing 100,000 transactions by a major tech company, while autonomously performing at least 3 of 5 specific functions (recommendations, complete purchases, order tracking, customer service, cross-vendor search). By late 2025, verified media and an earnings call statement from Amazon's CEO confirmed that Amazon's AI shopping assistant, Rufus, reached 250 million MAU. In late 2025, Rufus rolled out 'agentic' features including a 'Buy for Me' automatic purchasing capability. It now performs product recommendations, completes purchases using authorized methods, tracks orders, and handles customer service questions. Because these features strictly satisfy the conditions, and 250 million users is vastly larger than the 10,000 MAU threshold, the resolution criteria are extremely likely to be met and verified by Metaculus for Dec 31, 2026. (The 3 probabilities are provided to match the 3 items in the parsed 'N, /, A' resolution dates array).

As of mid-April 2026, the combined market capitalization of USDT and USDC is roughly $262.7 billion (USDT at ~$184.3 billion and USDC at ~$78.4 billion). In order to reach the $300 billion threshold by June 30, 2026, the combined market caps would need to grow by roughly $37.3 billion in just 2.5 months. This would require an average growth rate of about $15 billion per month. 

Recent historic data shows that over the past six months (between October 2025, when the total was ~$250 billion, and April 2026), the combined market cap only grew by approximately $12-13 billion in total, which equates to an average growth of ~$2 billion per month. Absent an unprecedented catalyst or extreme parabolic blow-off top causing massive instantaneous liquidity inflows, achieving a growth rate of 7x the recent monthly average over the coming weeks is highly unlikely. Considering these factors, I assign a very low probability of 6% to the combined market cap surpassing $300 billion by June 30, 2026.

The Supreme Court granted certiorari in Monsanto v. Durnell in mid-January 2026, setting oral arguments for April 2026. This timeline comfortably allows for a decision before the July 15, 2026 deadline (typically, SCOTUS terms conclude in late June or early July). The U.S. Solicitor General has aggressively backed Monsanto's position, reversing the government's previous stance, and a circuit split exists regarding whether FIFRA preempts state-level failure-to-warn claims. When the Supreme Court grants cert to resolve a circuit split with the Solicitor General's backing against a state court judgment, reversal rates are exceptionally high. Furthermore, the current 6-3 conservative majority is generally receptive to federal preemption arguments that protect corporations from state-level torts, particularly when a federal agency (the EPA) has explicitly approved the labeling at issue. In line with the community's elevated ~80% prediction, a favorable ruling for Monsanto is highly probable.

Based on the available evidence up to April 12, 2026, it is highly unlikely that an attempt will be made to fire Jerome Powell before his term ends. His term formally expires on May 15, 2026, and the deadline for this question is May 1, 2026, which is less than three weeks away from the forecast date. Earlier in 2025 and early 2026, Trump repeatedly criticized Powell and suggested he could be fired "for cause," but later officially clarified (such as in an exclusive January 2026 Reuters interview) that he had no intention of firing him despite ongoing investigations. Furthermore, Powell has indicated he will not resign, and his replacement is already being lined up for when his term expires naturally. Given the extremely short time horizon (18 days to the May 1 deadline) and the political and legal complexities of firing a Fed Chair so close to their scheduled departure, the probability is negligible. I'm assigning the minimum allowed threshold. (Note: The resolution dates array generated 3 items out of "N/A" due to a parsing bug, so I submitted three identical minimal probabilities).

The U.S.-Houthi ceasefire mediated by Oman on May 6, 2025, remains intact as of April 12, 2026. Both U.S. defense officials and independent monitors like ACLED confirm no deliberate U.S. strikes on Houthi targets or Houthi attacks on U.S. warships have occurred since the agreement began. While the Houthis 'belatedly' entered the active U.S.-Israel-Iran war on March 28, 2026, by resuming drone and missile strikes purely against Israel, they have explicitly avoided reopening the Red Sea shipping front or directly targeting U.S. assets to prevent devastating retaliation and to preserve their position in Yemen. With less than 50 days until the June 1, 2026 threshold and both sides demonstrating restraint toward each other despite the regional escalation, the likelihood of a direct, deliberate attack breaking the ceasefire in this short window is low.

The community's prediction was 83% on April 2, 2026, during the height of the escalation and 'Operation Epic Fury', with rumors of an impending US ground invasion of Kharg Island. However, recent developments dramatically limit the likelihood of a ground invasion. On April 7-8, 2026, the US and Iran agreed to a provisional two-week ceasefire, with Iran agreeing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Crucially, the US Department of War officially stated that Operation Epic Fury 'has achieved its objectives', indicating a strong preference to claim victory via airpower and diplomatic concessions rather than pursuing a perilous ground war. Military experts, IDF planning, and officials like the former CIA station chief have all forcefully cautioned against the massive logistical challenges, extreme summer heat, and high casualty potential associated with an invasion ('suicide mission'). While the truce is fragile and brokered by Pakistan, the immediate momentum for a US ground occupation has seemingly dissipated. Therefore, I have substantially lowered my probability to reflect this off-ramp, scaling probabilities slightly closer to 0.5 for subsequent variables down the line to account for the volatile long-term horizon of the ceasefire.

As of April 2026, 16 EU member states recognize Palestinian statehood (after France, Belgium, Luxembourg, Malta, and Portugal announced recognition in September 2025). To reach the resolution criteria of 19 states before July 2026, 3 out of the remaining 11 EU states would need to recognize Palestine. The 11 remaining states are Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Austria, Denmark, Finland, Greece, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, and Croatia. 

Currently, all 11 of these nations have explicitly stated they will not recognize Palestine unilaterally or at present, citing either a need for a negotiated settlement or general opposition. Furthermore, President Trump's strong opposition to the recognition of Palestine—including threatening trade deals with Canada for doing so—makes it exceptionally unlikely that closely US-aligned European states (like the Baltics, Germany, or Italy) would risk their trade relationships with Washington to cross this line before July 2026. Given the remaining states' firm political stances and substantial US diplomatic and economic pressure, reaching the 19-state threshold within the next two and a half months is virtually impossible. I am keeping my estimate lower than the April community average, estimating around 5-7%.

The IIE Fall 2025 Snapshot indicates that, comparing the 2025-2026 academic year to 2024-2025, undergraduate international student enrollments are up by 2% while graduate enrollments have decreased by 12%. The Snapshot includes data from over 825 US higher education institutions and serves as a highly robust predictor for the final Open Doors release in November 2026. Given the massive 14 percentage point gap (-12% vs +2%), it is extremely likely that the final report will confirm that international enrolled graduate students experienced a greater percentage decrease than undergraduates. I am predicting 95%, with slightly lower probabilities for subsequent dates per the instruction to increase uncertainty with horizon.

The only high-profile case positioned to challenge Obergefell (the Kim Davis case, Ermold v. Davis) had its cert petition denied by the Supreme Court in November 2025. There are currently no other known cases that have successfully reached the Supreme Court docket on this issue. Given that we are in April 2026, the timeline to introduce, argue, appeal, and file a new cert petition for any recently passed state laws (such as 'covenant marriage') before July 2026 is virtually impossible without extreme fast-tracking, for which there is no evidence. The community consensus was at 3% in early April, and checking recent SCOTUS cert grants up to late January/early spring 2026 confirms no such cases have been accepted or are strongly pending. Therefore, the chance of cert being granted before July 1, 2026, is at the floor (2%).

On February 23, 2026, Chad officially announced the "immediate and indefinite closure" of its eastern border with Sudan "until further notice." The announcement was made by Chad's Ministry of Information and the Communications Minister, Mahamat Gassim Cherif, explicitly citing repeated incursions by Sudanese armed groups and security precautions to prevent the conflict from spilling over. This strictly satisfies the resolution criteria: an official announcement by a neighboring country of a complete closure of all major border crossings with Sudan (specifically noting the entire eastern border and major crossings like Adre), explicitly citing the civil war and conflict spillover, and occurring well before the July 1, 2026 deadline. Since the event has already occurred, the probability is maximized at 0.98 for all dates.

Israel's participation in Eurovision 2026 was confirmed after the EBU assembly in December 2025 voted to allow them to compete without introducing a neutral flag. Several countries (Spain, Ireland, Netherlands, Slovenia, and Iceland) withdrew in protest. On March 5, 2026, Israel officially revealed their song, "Michelle," performed by Noam Bettan. The contest is just a month away (May 2026), making late disqualification or withdrawal highly unlikely, consistent with the community's 99% forecast on April 2, 2026. The new flag rules implemented in Austria apply to audience venue safety concerning all participant and non-participant flags, and do not impose a neutral flag on Israel's delegation.

The forecast horizon is short (under 7 months). Extensive web searches show no announcements or active development by any major coding tools (VS Code, JetBrains, Zed, Cursor) or specialized startups to release a Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) integration before November 2026. Current state-of-the-art BCI applications for computer control focus on OS-level cursor or keyboard emulation, sidestepping the need for IDE-specific integration. While academic prototypes exist (e.g., BCCLI), they have no commercialization roadmap. Therefore, the chance of a publicly accessible coding tool releasing such an integration by the deadline is exceedingly low, consistent with the community's 2% estimate.

The Supreme Court held second oral arguments in Louisiana v. Callais on October 15, 2025. This case centers on whether Louisiana's SB8 map (featuring two majority-Black congressional districts) is an unconstitutional racial gerrymander. The lower court already struck down the map, and the Supreme Court signaled deep skepticism toward it by ordering rare reargument concerning whether Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act permits or requires such race-conscious districting under the 14th and 15th Amendments. During the October 2025 arguments, conservative Justices (particularly Thomas, Alito, Gorsuch, and Kavanaugh) exhibited hostility toward the race-conscious remedy and suggested imposing temporal limits on the VRA. Furthermore, the State of Louisiana itself supports striking down the map. Given the 6-3 conservative supermajority, the tone of oral arguments, the fact that striking down the map merely requires affirming the lower court's finding of a lack of narrow tailoring, and expert consensus that the Court seems "ready to curtail [a] major provision of the Voting Rights Act," it is highly likely (around 87%) that the Court will hold the map invalid before the end of the 2025-2026 term in June 2026.

The community prediction was 3% in early April, and new information reinforces this low probability. The goal to map a >1 exabyte connectome (specifically the whole mouse brain) by June 2031 is currently expected to be technologically feasible but faces funding headwinds. Argonne’s Aurora exascale supercomputer is actively being prepped for this massive dataset. However, a major 2023 Wellcome Trust report estimated a whole mouse connectome on a 10-15 year timeline (2033+). Furthermore, significant funding cuts to the NIH BRAIN Initiative ($278M in late 2024) and the cancellation of specific Harvard multi-site connectome scaling grants in 2025 demonstrate that purely public U.S. efforts may be delayed. The question resolves YES if New People (NL) achieves the largest net seat gain in the 2026 Russian State Duma election. Current polling shows United Russia and the Communist Party (CPRF) losing support compared to 2021, meaning they will likely lose seats. The two main parties poised to gain seats are the Liberal Democratic Party of Russia (LDPR) and New People (NL). Polling indicates both could see ~40-50 seat gains. However, LDPR consistently edges out NL in polls (e.g., 13.3% vs 11.1%), and importantly, LDPR has historically secured single-mandate district seats while NL won zero in 2021. Additionally, the tiebreaker rules favor the party with more valid votes, heavily leaning toward LDPR based on current projections. Thus, LDPR is the clear favorite to gain the most seats, making NL a plausible but distinct underdog. The Polymarket price of 24.5% perfectly reflects these dynamics.

The Democratic party retains an overwhelming advantage in California's 38th district. In 2024, Democratic incumbent Linda Sanchez won the district by 19.6 points. Although Sanchez is running in the 41st district in 2026 due to Proposition 50 redistricting, the 38th district remains solidly blue. Hilda Solis, a prominent and popular Democrat (former US Secretary of Labor and current LA County Supervisor), is running for the open CA-38 seat. Furthermore, Prop 50 was explicitly designed by Democrats to increase their partisan advantage across California, meaning they would not have redrawn CA-38 to be vulnerable to a GOP flip, particularly not when a heavyweight like Solis intends to run there. The market price for this seat is accurately pegged at 0.95. Short of an unforeseen scandal or catastrophic polling miss in heavily Democratic Los Angeles County areas, this seat will reliably stay in the Democratic column.

Nino Batsiashvili was born in January 1987, making her 39 years old currently (as of April 2026). Her rating on the April 2026 list is 2472. A 1% increase requires her to reach a rating of at least 2496.72 (effectively 2497). While she achieved a peak rating of 2528 in 2018, her rating has stabilized around 2460 in recent years, making a 25-point jump less likely in the short term, especially as classical chess players typically see a slow Elo decline in their late 30s and 40s. Additionally, FIDE lists update monthly, meaning there is effectively zero chance of her crossing this threshold for the immediate April 2026 resolution date, and extremely low chances for May. However, as the forecast horizon extends into the late 2020s and 2030s, the uncertainty significantly increases. This is due to potential variance in performance over long time horizons and the possibility of FIDE rating inflation or structural adjustments over the next decade. Following instructions to increase uncertainty for distant dates, the probability gracefully scales from 0.05 directly towards 0.50.

There is currently no approved human vaccine for Yersiniosis (typically caused by Yersinia enterocolitica or Yersinia pseudotuberculosis). Research is limited to preclinical stages, such as in silico modeling and mouse studies (e.g., cross-protective effects from experimental Yersinia pestis/plague vaccines). Since there are no human clinical trials currently underway for a specific Yersiniosis vaccine, the likelihood of a vaccine being fully developed, approved, and recognized on Wikipedia in the short term (2026-2029) is exceptionally low. The very low short-term probabilities (2-4%) largely account for the baseline risk of a Wikipedia editor incorrectly updating the page based on veterinary vaccines (like Yersiniavax for deer) or experimental data. For the longer-term dates (2031, 2036), the probability rises to 11-20% to account for standard vaccine development timelines (which usually take 10+ years from the preclinical phase) and the possibility that an experimental cross-protective vaccine for plague might eventually be approved and broadly classified as protecting against Yersiniosis.

The Wikipedia article currently says "No" for Trichuriasis vaccine as of April 2026. The literature review shows that no vaccines have even entered clinical trials as of early 2026. A trial establishing a Controlled Human Infection Model (CHIM) is just starting or ongoing as of late 2025/early 2026. Vaccine development for helminths is notoriously difficult, taking typically 10-15+ years from Phase 1 to approval. Since there is currently no Phase 1 candidate, it is exceedingly unlikely that a vaccine will be approved and incorporated into Wikipedia before 2036. The probabilities for the near-term dates (2026-2027) are extremely low (0.05 minimum allowed), and the probabilities for 2029-2036 increase slightly to account for the possibility of very rapid development or an unexpected breakthrough, but remain very low (0.12 by 2036).

Kateryna Lagno's Elo rating on Wikipedia as of April 12, 2026, is exactly 2508. A 1% increase requires her rating to hit at least 2534 (2533.08). The Wikipedia page derives its table directly from the official FIDE monthly lists, which are only updated on the 1st of every month. Thus, on April 19, her rating will definitely still reflect the April 1 list (2508). For May 12, she will have completed the 2026 Women's Candidates Tournament, which ends April 16. Sepsis is a complex, multi-pathogen syndrome, making a universal vaccine biologically extremely difficult. While recent news from April 2026 highlights preclinical or early-stage development of a composite vaccine by Longhorn Vaccines targeting common bacterial toxins (LPS/LTA), clinical trials for such novel candidates typically take many years with a high failure rate. Considering the resolution relies on a Wikipedia edit to the 'List of infectious diseases' recognizing a sepsis vaccine as 'created,' immediate updates depend purely on premature edits, which are unlikely to persist or meet the standard. In the short term (2026-2027), the probability is practically constrained to the minimum allowed bound (0.05) due to the impossibly short timeline for any legitimate vaccine rollout. Over longer horizons (2031, 2036), the probability scales up (0.15, 0.25) largely as a representation of increasing uncertainty and the small but non-zero chance that a broad-spectrum anti-toxin or immunomodulatory vaccine clears Phase 3 trials and gets adopted into standard medical definitions over a 10-year period.

There is currently no human vaccine for Clostridium perfringens food poisoning, nor are there any registered human clinical trials as of 2026. The disease is generally mild and self-limiting, which significantly reduces the clinical need and financial incentive for vaccine development in humans (although veterinary vaccines for livestock exist). Because vaccine development and approval typically take 8-15 years, it is virtually impossible to have an approved vaccine mapped to Wikipedia by 2031. Any positive resolution in the near term would likely be due to temporary Wikipedia vandalism or errors, which carries a baseline probability of roughly 2%. For the most distant date (2036), there is a very small non-zero chance that early-stage research (such as recent bivalent or mRNA candidates) progresses to a successful vaccine, which brings the probability up to 5%.

Rapport's most recently known ranking (as of Wikipedia on Apr 2, 2026) is 19. Since conventional English treats "higher ranking" as a numerically smaller position (e.g., #1 is the highest rank), the condition requires his rank to be 19th or better (or match whatever exact rank he holds on April 12). 

1. Very short-term (April 19): Wikipedia is unlikely to undergo major table reshuffling since official FIDE list updates occur at the start of the month. Thus, his probability of maintaining his current parsed rank within 1 week is very high.
2. Short to Medium-term (May to Oct 2026): Top players' ratings will fluctuate due to major events like the April Candidates tournament. Rapport's rank of #19 is quite precarious and he recently dropped into the upper 20s in late 2025 before bouncing back. Half of the top 20 players are vulnerable to small rating swings, making his position volatile, decreasing probability of holding <=19.
3. Long-term (Years 1-10): Forecasting the exact rank of a 30-year-old player with high volatility amidst rising teenage prodigies makes long-term certainty impossible. Following the forecasting guidelines, uncertainty maximizes for distant dates, tapering probabilities mathematically towards 0.5.

There are currently no clinical trials or public health campaigns prioritizing a vaccine for Lymphocytic choriomeningitis (LCMV). LCMV is generally a rare, mild infection in humans (except for vertical transmission and in immunocompromised transplant recipients) and lacks the commercial and epidemic drivers typically required to fund multi-year human trials. Many recent papers use 'LCMV' in the context of it being a viral vector (e.g., used to deliver TB or CMV antigens) rather than being the target of the vaccine itself. Given standard vaccine development timelines (typically 10+ years without rapid prioritization), there is virtually zero chance of an approved human vaccine appearing by 2026-2029. The main chance for an affirmative resolution is if a Wikipedia editor erroneously adds 'Yes' due to confusion with LCMV-vectored vaccines, adds an experimental animal model vaccine, or engages in vandalism. Over longer horizons (2031-2036), the probability slightly increases to account for general uncertainty and standard horizon effects, though it remains securely below 10%.

Le Quang Liem’s current FIDE Elo from the April 1, 2026 list is 2731. A 1% increase requires an Elo of 2759, which translates to moving into the top 6 in the world. Liem, currently 35 (born 1991) and semi-active as an elite classical player mostly due to his coaching duties at Webster University, has never achieved this rating. For the short-term horizons (2026), reaching 2759 is virtually impossible, particularly for April 19, which will rely on the static April list. Jumping 28 points would necessitate winning multiple super-tournaments overwhelmingly. As the horizon stretches to 1, 3, 5, and 10 years, factors like potential Elo rating inflation increase the upside risk, though counterbalanced by the fact that he will age into his 40s (players typically see classical rating declines past 40). Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase with horizon and shift towards 0.5, probabilities are steeply graded from 5% up to 40% for 2036.

The development of a vaccine for Onchocerciasis (River blindness) is still in the extremely early stages. Recent scientific literature from late 2024 and October 2025 indicates that the leading vaccine candidates (such as Ov-RAL-2, Ov-103, and the Ov-FUS-1 fusion) are only just preparing for Phase 1 clinical trials. A November 2025 news article also highlights a $3.6 million grant specifically to fund TOVA's Phase 1 clinical trials. 

Vaccine development typically takes 10 to 15 years from the start of Phase 1 to full regulatory approval and public availability, with high attrition rates along the way. Given that Phase 1 trials have not yet completed (or possibly not even started) as of early 2026, it is virtually impossible for a vaccine to be successfully "created" (meaning approved and recognized by Wikipedia as a definitive vaccine rather than just an experimental candidate) at any point in 2026, 2027, or even 2029. Therefore, I place the minimum allowed probability (0.02) on all dates up to 2029. 

For 2031, it would represent a very aggressive 5-year timeline from Phase 1 to full approval, which is possible but highly unlikely for a neglected tropical disease, so I place the probability at 0.05. By 2036 (a 10-year horizon), it is more plausible that a vaccine could complete Phase 3 trials and receive approval if trials are successful, so the probability rises to 0.15 reflecting this possibility.

Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) is a broad clinical syndrome caused by an ascending infection from multiple possible distinct microorganisms, primarily Chlamydia trachomatis and Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Given that it is not a single specific infectious agent but a spectrum of disease caused by various bacteria, a single "vaccine for PID" does not fit the typical biomedical paradigm. Currently, no approved vaccine exists for either Chlamydia or Gonorrhea, and though early-stage research is ongoing, neither is expected to have an approved vaccine in the near term. Even if a vaccine for one of the common causal pathogens is eventually approved, it would almost certainly be documented on Wikipedia under the specific pathogen (e.g., Chlamydia) rather than generic PID. Due to the biological reality of this multicausal syndrome and the fact that vaccine development pipelines for its principal agents are far from realization, the probability of a "PID vaccine" being developed and verified on the Wikipedia page by 2036 is extremely low. The probabilities increase slightly for the later dates to represent rising uncertainty over long forecast horizons, as per instructions.

Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) is a broad clinical syndrome caused by an ascending infection from multiple possible distinct microorganisms, primarily Chlamydia trachomatis and Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Given that it is not a single specific infectious agent but a spectrum of disease caused by various bacteria, a single "vaccine for PID" does not fit the typical biomedical paradigm. Currently, no approved vaccine exists for either Chlamydia or Gonorrhea, and though early-stage research is ongoing, neither is expected to have an approved vaccine in the near term. Even if a vaccine for one of the common causal pathogens is eventually approved, it would almost certainly be documented on Wikipedia under the specific pathogen (e.g., Chlamydia) rather than generic PID. Due to the biological reality of this multicausal syndrome and the fact that vaccine development pipelines for its principal agents are far from realization, the probability of a "PID vaccine" being developed and verified on the Wikipedia page by 2036 is extremely low. The probabilities increase slightly for the later dates to represent rising uncertainty over long forecast horizons, as per instructions.

Lei Tingjie's official ranking on the Wikipedia FIDE page as of April 1, 2026, is #2 (rating 2566). To be 'as high or higher', she must remain #1 or #2 on the respective resolution dates. 
For April 19, 2026, the Wikipedia page will almost certainly still display the April 1st FIDE list, guaranteeing her rank remains 2. 
For May 12, the new May 1st rating list will be out. The primary threat to her rank is #4 Zhu Jiner (2554) playing the 2026 Women's Candidates. However, live results show Zhu Jiner scoring 6/11, perfectly aligning with her expected score against a ~2513 field. Thus, Zhu will not gain the ~12 points needed to surpass Lei. #3 Ju Wenjun (2559) is not playing in the Candidates and has no clear path to suddenly gain +7 points before May, leaving Lei highly likely to secure #2.
Over the next few months to a year (July 2026 - April 2027), there is increasing risk of Ju Wenjun, Zhu Jiner, or bouncing competitors like Goryachkina overtaking her. 
Over the multi-year horizons (2029-2036), staying in the top 2 will be extremely difficult due to the rise of young prodigies (e.g., Divya Deshmukh, Vaishali Rameshbabu, Lu Miaoyi) who are rapidly gaining ELO. Lei will be 32 in 2029 and 39 in 2036, making it deeply unlikely she maintains a top-2 spot a decade from now.

Hou Yifan is ranked #1 in the FIDE Top 100 Women list as of April 1, 2026, with a rating of 2596. Her nearest competitor, Lei Tingjie, is at 2566 (a 30-point gap). FIDE updates its list monthly on the 1st. 

For 2026-04-19, the FIDE list will be identical to the April 1st list, guaranteeing she remains #1. For 2026-05-12, bridging a 30-point gap in one month requires an extraordinary performance (e.g., scoring 11/14 in the upcoming Candidates), making it highly probable Hou remains #1. 

However, as time progresses, Hou's limited playing schedule and gradual rating decline (from a peak of 2686 down to the current 2596) work against her. Meanwhile, active competitors like Lei Tingjie and Zhu Jiner have increasing opportunities to climb past her. Additionally, FIDE's inactivity rules (which remove players who haven't played a rated game in 12 months from the active ranking list) pose a significant risk if she plays even less in the future. By 1 year (April 2027), the gap may well be closed, giving her roughly a 58% chance of retaining the top spot. By 3-10 years, her semi-retiree status and the emergence of talented younger players make it extremely likely that she will either fall in ranking or drop off the list due to inactivity entirely.

The infectious agent for Kawasaki disease remains unknown (as noted on the Wikipedia article itself), which makes developing a targeted vaccine virtually impossible at this stage. Even if the pathogen were to be discovered tomorrow, the typical vaccine development pipeline (including preclinical research, safety testing, Phase 1-3 clinical trials, and regulatory approval) typically takes 10+ years. Furthermore, Kawasaki disease is relatively rare, meaning a vaccine would not receive the emergency, accelerated funding seen during acute pandemics. Web searches verify that current medical literature discussing "Kawasaki disease" and "vaccines" is exclusively focused on whether other routine childhood vaccines could be potential triggers for the syndrome, or on how well existing vaccines work following standard immunoglobulin treatments for the disease. There are no ongoing vaccine development programs for Kawasaki disease itself. Therefore, the chance of a vaccine being successfully developed and documented on the specific Wikipedia page by the resolution dates is extremely minimal. I will provide a baseline 5% probability for the short and medium-term horizons (to respect the minimum allowed bounds), slowly increasing to 9% at the 10-year horizon to account for increasing uncertainty and possible (though highly improbable) scientific breakthroughs over a decade.

The infectious agent for Kawasaki disease remains unknown (as noted on the Wikipedia article itself), which makes developing a targeted vaccine virtually impossible at this stage. Even if the pathogen were to be discovered tomorrow, the typical vaccine development pipeline (including preclinical research, safety testing, Phase 1-3 clinical trials, and regulatory approval) typically takes 10+ years. Furthermore, Kawasaki disease is relatively rare, meaning a vaccine would not receive the emergency, accelerated funding seen during acute pandemics. Web searches verify that current medical literature discussing "Kawasaki disease" and "vaccines" is exclusively focused on whether other routine childhood vaccines could be potential triggers for the syndrome, or on how well existing vaccines work following standard immunoglobulin treatments for the disease. Anish Giri's official Elo on April 12, 2026, is 2753. A 1% jump requires reaching 2781. FIDE list updates are monthly, so the April 19 rating will definitively stay 2753. Gaining 28+ Elo points implies performing exceptionally well across multiple elite tournaments (earning roughly +6 above expected score). Although Giri is doing well at the active 2026 Candidates with an estimated +10 points to his live rating, closing the remaining gap to 2781 by May-October 2026 is highly improbable given his tendency to hold rating across normal supertournaments. Moving into the 1 to 3 year forecasts, there's a higher chance he could hit a hot streak to reclaim a top-3 level (reminiscent of recent surges by peers like Nakamura or Caruana), pushing probabilities incrementally higher. For very distant forecasts (2031, 2036), Giri will be in his late 30s to early 40s. Age-related decline would typically keep him from 2781 without significant FIDE rating deflation adjustments or broader inflation, pulling probabilities heavily toward absolute uncertainty.

The question asks whether Alina Kashlinskaya will have a FIDE rank of #15 or better (as high or higher) on several future dates. She is currently ranked #15 on the April 2026 list with a rating of 2480. 
For 2026-04-19, the FIDE rankings on Wikipedia will almost certainly not have updated since lists are published monthly on the 1st. Thus, she will still be #15.
For the subsequent 2026 dates (May, July, October), she is in a highly competitive bracket (the players ranked #13 to #17 are within 15 points of each other). She has roughly a coin-flip chance of remaining in the top 15 each month, perhaps dipping slightly below 0.5 over time as younger rising stars (like Carissa Yip, Divya Deshmukh) continue to improve.
For the long term (2027 to 2036), Kashlinskaya will age from 32 to almost 43. Given the historical trajectory of female chess players and the influx of new talent, it is highly likely her ranking will gradually fall outside the top 15. The probabilities are kept moderately conservative to account for potential resurgences, but reflect a clear downward trend in the likelihood of her maintaining a peak ranking.

The query resolves based on the "Vaccine(s)" column of the "List of infectious diseases" Wikipedia page. Upon reviewing this Wikipedia page, the column is currently completely blank for Chytridiomycosis, as it is for almost all other diseases on the list (including COVID-19, Influenza, and Hepatitis). This severely depresses the baseline probability, because even if a vaccine were to be developed, there is a very high chance the Wikipedia table would still remain blank due to editor neglect. 

In the real world, there is currently no commercially available or approved vaccine for Chytridiomycosis. However, there is active research, most notably by Anthony Waddle in Australia, who is experimenting with a "transmissible vaccine" via weakened chytrid strains as of early 2026. For this research to translate into a "Yes" on the specified Wikipedia table, it needs to be proven effective, widely recognized as a "vaccine," and an editor must specifically update the obscure Chytridiomycosis row in the overarching "List of infectious diseases" page. 

Given these dual hurdles—wildlife vaccine commercialization and Wikipedia table maintenance—the probability of resolution remaining "No" is extremely high in the short term. Over longer horizons (5-10 years), the chance of a successful vaccine and a corresponding Wikipedia update increases slightly, but remains low.

For this research to translate into a "Yes" on the specified Wikipedia table, it needs to be proven effective, widely recognized as a "vaccine," and an editor must specifically update the obscure Chytridiomycosis row in the overarching "List of infectious diseases" page. 

Given these dual hurdles—wildlife vaccine commercialization and Wikipedia table maintenance—the probability of resolution remaining "No" is extremely high in the short term. Over longer horizons (5-10 years), the chance of a successful vaccine and a corresponding Wikipedia update increases slightly, but remains low.

The development of a vaccine for Paracoccidioidomycosis (PCM) is highly unlikely within the next 5-10 years. Currently, there are no approved human vaccines for any fungal infection. While research is ongoing (such as the P10 peptide vaccine), it is largely confined to preclinical murine models and is intended as a therapeutic adjuvant rather than a preventative vaccine. A broad-spectrum pan-fungal vaccine (from UGA) is just entering Phase 1 clinical trials as of mid-2025, but vaccine development typically takes 10-15 years minimum to move from Phase 1 to approval, particularly for neglected tropical diseases which chronically suffer from underfunding and logistical hurdles in conducting large efficacy trials. Therefore, the chance of a vaccine being developed, approved, and recorded on Wikipedia is effectively zero for the near-term dates (2026-2029). By 2031, there is a marginal chance (5%) if early trials are fast-tracked or mRNA technology accelerates timelines drastically. By 2036, there is a slightly higher chance (12%) that broader fungal vaccines entering trials today will have come to fruition and include coverage for PCM.

As of April 2, 2026, Nana Dzagnidze is ranked #19 on the FIDE women's list published on Wikipedia. For the first resolution date (April 19), no new FIDE list will have been released, meaning the page will perfectly reflect the April 1 rankings and her rank will remain #19, satisfying the condition. However, for May 2026 and beyond, new lists will be published. At age 39, Dzagnidze is in the twilight of her absolute peak (historically reaching #3 with a 2573 rating, now down to 2469). She sits precariously at #19, with Harika Dronavalli (#20) just 3 points behind, alongside many rapidly climbing younger players like Divya Deshmukh. Over the next 1-12 months, she faces a strong probability of dropping to #20 or lower, pushing our estimates slightly below 0.5. As we forecast further out (2029-2036), typical aging curves strongly suggest she will drop out of the top 20 entirely. However, honoring the constraint to let profound long-term ambiguity reflect in probabilities closer to 0.5 (e.g., changes to Wikipedia's formatting, FIDE system updates, or unexpected career revivals), the probabilities revert slowly back towards a coin toss for the 3-10 year horizons.

Hikaru Nakamura was ranked #2 on the April 2026 FIDE list (the benchmark). For his ranking to be "as high or higher," he must maintain rank #1 or #2. 
- 2026-04-19 (0.96): Wikipedia listings update monthly; the April list will still be visible. Even if updated live, Nakamura recently regained the #2 live spot (approx. 2804 to Caruana's 2795) by defeating Caruana in Round 8 of the Candidates 2026, making a YES near certain.
- 2026-05-12 (0.77): The May FIDE rating list will include the rest of the April Candidates. With a ~9 point lead over Caruana and 6 games remaining, Nakamura has roughly a 75-80% chance to hold onto the #2 rank.
- 2026-07-11 to 2027-04-12 (0.63 to 0.42): As time passes, holding a 2800+ rating becomes increasingly precarious. Rising prodigies like Abdusattorov (2780) and Firouzja, as well as a resilient Caruana, present constant threats. Probabilities drop below 0.5 in late 2026.
- 2029 to 2036 (0.31 to 0.18): Generational change in chess makes it incredibly unlikely for an older player to retain the #2 spot indefinitely. Nakamura will turn 40 in 2027 and will be 48 in 2036. While empirical base rates for a 48-year-old being rank #2 are almost 0, the probabilities for distant dates stay closer to 0.5 (as per instructions to reflect increasing uncertainty).

There is currently no approved vaccine for histoplasmosis (or any fungal infection in humans). A review of recent literature and clinical trial databases (up to April 2026) shows that research is confined to animal models (such as mice) and immunoinformatics. A recent publication (Nov 2025) explicitly noted that human fungal vaccines remain 'so far off'. Given that developing and approving a novel vaccine typically takes at least 10 years from the onset of phase I clinical trials—and no phase I trials have even begun for histoplasmosis—the probability of an approved vaccine being listed on Wikipedia in the next 1-5 years is virtually zero. By 2036, there is a small chance that mRNA or other novel platforms could expedite the development of a candidate, but the probability remains low. I am setting the near-term probabilities to the minimum bound of 5%, rising slightly by the 2036 horizon.

There are currently no vaccines in clinical trials or active commercial development, and reverse genetics systems to even study the virus have only just been established in early 2025. Given the typical 10-15 year development timeline for vaccines, the lack of commercial incentive, and the current absence of any vaccine candidate in human trials, the probability of a vaccine being created and listed on Wikipedia in the near term (2026-2027) is extremely low. Probabilities increase slightly for the 2029-2036 horizon to account for unforeseen advancements in rapid vaccine platforms (like mRNA) and general long-term uncertainty, but remain quite low overall.

The resolution requires Javokhir Sindarov to be ranked 11th or better on the specified dates. For the first date (April 19, 2026), the official FIDE list will not have updated yet (updates happen universally on the 1st of every month), so the April 1st list maintaining him at 11th will still be current. For May 2026 and beyond, we look at his recent live performance: Sindarov is dominating the Candidates 2026 tournament with 8.5/11 and has pushed his live ranking up by 14 Elo points to 2759, which gives him a live world rank of #6. Barring an apocalyptic 3-loss streak in the last three rounds, he is virtually guaranteed to be in the FIDE top 10 when the May 1 list is released. 

Moving into mid/late 2026, he is likely to participate in high-level closed tournaments and potentially a World Championship match, during which top players generally protect or boost their rating. However, the rating band from 2740-2770 is dense, heavily populated by young rising stars like Gukesh, Firouzja, Praggnanandhaa, and Keymer. Given this density, rank volatility is somewhat high — bad form for a single tournament could cost someone their top-11 status. Despite this volatility, Sindarov is just 20 years old and showing massive breakout strength, making it very probable he stays elite overall. 

Over a multi-year horizon (1 to 10 years), the inherent variance of chess careers (form changes, new prodigies, shifting meta) comes into play. While most prodigies establishing themselves in the top 10 around age 20 maintain a spot for several years, there is enough historical precedent of top-10 players dropping out to temper extreme confidence. In line with the prompt’s guideline, uncertainty properly increases as the forecast horizon extends out to 2036, trending toward 0.5.

FIDE releases official rating lists on the 1st of each month. The 2026-04-12 rating was 2482. A 1% increase requires a rating of >= 2507. For 2026-04-19, the FIDE rating will be identical to the April 1 list (2482), virtually guaranteeing the condition is not met (p=0.02). For 2026-05-12, she would need to gain 25 rating points in a single month; recent data suggests she played in the American Cup in March (likely accounted for in her April rating) and such a leap in April alone is unlikely (0.05). In the medium-to-long term, Carissa is young, the US #1 woman, and has stated intentions to pursue chess professionally. Gaining 25 points over 1-3 years is plausible, leading to a steady increase in probability. By 2029-2036, there are huge uncertainties around plateauing (many top women historically hover around 2450-2500), retirement, and the risk that if she reaches 2507 she still might not make the Wikipedia Top 20 table due to rating inflation. Therefore, probabilities naturally increase but cap out at maximum uncertainty (0.50) for the most distant horizons according to instructions.

The forecasted threshold is 312.57, which was AON's market closing price on the last active trading day (April 10, 2026) prior to the April 12 baseline date. AON saw a somewhat jarring 4% drop down to this 312.57 level on the 10th. However, on April 11, AON announced a 10% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.82/share, a bullish signal for the market. Additionally, Barclays maintained an "Equal-Weight" rating while adjusting its price target down to $372 — which still represents a massive premium (~19%) over the 312.57 threshold. The statistical base-rate model outputs a 59% chance of the stock remaining above 312.60 by April 19 organically. Given the dividend increase and the likelihood of mean reversion after a sharp 1-day dip, the near-term probability is estimated at 0.62 for April 19. 

Per the explicit and strict instructions of the system prompt assigning that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5", the sequence has been intentionally modeled to gradually converge toward 0.51 for the longest horizon (2036). While typical stock market dynamics often imply greater positive long-term drift and probabilities that grow to ~0.7-0.8+ over very long multi-year horizons, we strictly follow the prompt constraint requiring uncertainty decay toward 0.5.

Ford (F) stock recently closed at $12.13 (as of April 10, 2026) following a significant 6% jump on April 8th driven by strong Q1 truck/SUV sales and corporate partnership announcements. Because rapid short-term jumps in non-growth, dividend-paying legacy stocks frequently mean-revert or stall in the subsequent days and weeks, the probability of the price staying above $12.13 in the immediate term is slightly below 50%. The statistical tool analysis also estimated the probability of exceeding this peak at 1-week to be around 40-46%. As the forecast horizon increases, long-term uncertainty naturally dilutes this short-term technical signal. Ford's historical price action is largely flat over multi-year periods due to high dividend payouts, which suppress nominal price growth compared to non-dividend growth stocks. Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase and long-term probabilities should heavily converge to 0.5, I smoothly scale probabilities from 0.42 at the one-week mark toward exactly 0.50 for the 5-year and 10-year outlooks, reflecting maximum long-run uncertainty on the nominal price of this specific stock.

The short-term trend for GEV shows downward momentum (P(increase) = 0.44 according to the yfinance combined estimate), alongside an initially strong linear trend since it began trading. The short-term probability is anchored around 0.44 reflecting recent negative momentum. According to general stock market dynamics, individual stock drift becomes harder to predict over months and years, but typically leans slightly positive due to long-term market returns. Aligning with the requirement to increase uncertainty for distant forecasting horizons and keep probabilities closer to 0.5, the probabilities smoothly regress from the 0.44 short-term anchor towards 0.50 at the 1-3 year horizon, and edge marginally above 0.50 (to 0.51) up to the 10-year horizon reflecting minor long-term equity growth expectancy while maintaining maximum uncertainty.

Corning's stock has surged dramatically over the past year (up nearly 285%), reaching a recent high of $171.24. This massive rally has been fueled by AI enthusiasm and a new $6B multiyear fiber deal with Meta. However, signs point to current overvaluation: the stock's P/E ratio is over 93, analysts' recent price targets (e.g., Mizuho's raise to $160) are still well below the current market price, and a DCF analysis implies it may be overvalued by around 28%. The time series trend model provides a combined base rate estimate of 0.40 for the stock staying above this elevated threshold in the short term, predicting a reversion. Given these factors, the probability of the stock closing higher than $171.24 in the near future is evaluated to be moderately below 50% due to the high likelihood of a near-term correction or consolidation. In accordance with the instruction that uncertainty should increase as the horizon grows longer, the probabilities steadily increase towards 0.50 for distant dates (up to 10 years out), reflecting the fact that long-term price action becomes dominated by broad market drift and macro factors rather than current overvaluation.

However, signs point to current overvaluation: the stock's P/E ratio is over 93, analysts' recent price targets (e.g., Mizuho's raise to $160) are still well below the current market price, and a DCF analysis implies it may be overvalued by around 28%. The time series trend model provides a combined base rate estimate of 0.40 for the stock staying above this elevated threshold in the short term, predicting a reversion. Given these factors, the probability of the stock closing higher than $171.24 in the near future is evaluated to be moderately below 50% due to the high likelihood of a near-term correction or consolidation. In accordance with the instruction that uncertainty should increase as the horizon grows longer, the probabilities steadily increase towards 0.50 for distant dates (up to 10 years out), reflecting the fact that long-term price action becomes dominated by broad market drift and macro factors rather than current overvaluation.

The forecasting strategy uses the output of the requested historical statistical tool as the primary base rate anchor. The tool provided a combined probability estimate of P(exceeds current price) = 0.43 for the immediate term (Apr 19), mostly dragged by negative seasonality comparing to prior years. Our single web search revealed strong positive fundamental news out of early 2026, finding that Generac has posted a series of wins regarding its data center-focused outlook and new acquisitions (e.g., Enercon). This qualitative news offers a modest bullish offset to the tool's 0.43 estimate, prompting us to anchor the short-term prediction at 0.44. In strict accordance with the instructions, uncertainty dominates across progressively larger time horizons because long-run individual stock paths exhibit massive compounding variance, pulling the binomial choice probability closer to the neutral 0.5. As such, the probabilities smoothly converge to 0.50 from 1 week out (0.44) through 10 years out (0.50).

Hess Corporation (HES) was acquired by Chevron and delisted from the NYSE on July 18, 2025. The resolution criteria explicitly state: "Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price." This hardcodes the resolution value to the stock's final trading price (which appears to be 148.970) for all dates after the delisting. Accordingly, the market close price for the base date (2026-04-12) resolves to the final close price, and the price on each subsequent resolution date also resolves to the exact same final close price. Since the question asks if the price will be strictly "higher" than the price on 2026-04-12, and the value will remain exactly the same, the condition for resolving YES (Price > Price) cannot be met. Probabilities are therefore set at the minimum allowed limit (0.05) for the near-term, and slightly higher for distant dates to strictly comply with the rule that uncertainty must increase over long time horizons (to account for minor edge cases such as platform rule changes, relisting under the exact same ticker, or ambiguous resolution).

The current threshold price (based on the latest 2026-04-10 close) is 96.07. 

Short-term (1 week / 1 month): The stock has experienced a small local bump in early April (up from a ~94.50 average to 96.07). The linear and seasonal models from historical data estimate P(> 96.07) at 0.39-0.45, reflecting slight downward momentum from earlier in 2026 and potential mean reversion. Following the instruction to keep weekly moves close to 0.5, I set 1-week at 0.46 and 1-month at 0.48, slightly favoring a reversion from the recent short-term peak.

Medium-term (3 months - 1 year): Incyte reported solid Q4 2025 earnings (+28% year-over-year revenue growth) and released positive late-stage clinical data in March 2026 for povorcitinib. With a 1-year consensus analyst price target of ~$106, the expected value shifts above the current price over a 12-month timeframe, so the probability climbs to 0.53 for the 1-year horizon.

Long-term (3+ years): The prompt instructs that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5. Individual biotech stocks carry significant specific risk (such as the long-term expiration of key patents like Jakafi and clinical trial outcomes) that offsets the general upward drift of the broader stock market. Consequently, the long-term probabilities regress steadily down to 0.50 over the 3-year, 5-year, and 10-year horizons.

The baseline statistical model estimates a P(value > 150.3) of exactly 0.40 for the coming week, noting strong recent downward trends since Jan 2026, though there was a brief bounce over the final three days leading up to the cutoff. The consensus earnings expectations (due April 21) are relatively muted and Wells Fargo has lowered its price target to $160, signaling caution on the stock. Given the mandate that uncertainty should increase as the horizon grows (pushing predictions for further dates closer to 0.5), I start slightly below 50% for the near-term forecasts to respect the downward momentum and statistical prior, and then gradually adjust the probabilities back towards the maximal uncertainty of 0.5 or exactly 0.5 for multi-year horizons. Since single-stock long-term returns are highly volatile and their positive skew makes the median return often lower than the mean, a 50% probability is well justified for 10-year outlooks under maximum uncertainty directives.
